items.title: Communities 'in the middle of nowhere' are scared they'll run out of fuel

children.content: Remote communities are calling for assurance on fuel security in the face of potential shortages, along with financial assistance as already-high fuel and food prices continue to climb.

image.altText: Alastair King stands at the checkout register of a small grocery store, speaking to the worker behind the counter.

items.title: Hospital staff using ChatGPT to calculate medication doses, nurses claim

children.content: Whistleblowers say understaffing at the Northern Territory's busiest hospital is forcing nurses to take on unsafe patient loads, and some are teaching themselves medical procedures via YouTube.

image.altText: A nurse in uniform walking through the corridor of a hospital, facing away from the camera.

items.title: World's biggest live export ship transports cattle out of Darwin after being anchored for weeks

children.content: Anchored off Fremantle for weeks because the war in Iran had stopped the live sheep trade to the Middle East, the world's largest live export vessel has ventured north to Darwin.

gallery.altText: MV Al Kuwait at the Darwin Port

thumbnail.altText: MV Al Kuwait at the Darwin Port

items.title: Not all city suburbs have NBN access and it isn't by accident. Here's why

children.content: Despite popular belief, not all Australians are guaranteed access to the NBN — and as many consumers have discovered, alternative networks do not always provide quality service.

image.altText: A graphic showing a person's hands on a laptop with a Wifi failure symbol superimposed over the top.

avatar.alt: An Australian-Egyptian woman with dark brown shoulder-length curly hair smiling at camera, in a green patterned top.

items.title: NT councils flag cuts to street lights, pool hours as result of power prices

children.content: Power price hikes are expected to be passed onto local councils by the Northern Territory government in the coming month, with Darwin's lord mayor warning some community services could be cut as a result.

image.altText: A sign outside an old building reads: City of Darwin.

items.title: Flood-damaged roads cause 'catastrophic' impacts for NT businesses

children.content: Workers in road-reliant industries are calling on the federal government to provide urgent funding to repair the NT's road network, which was severely damaged by floods.

gallery.altText: A drone shot shows a damaged dirt road alongside a brown river, with a massive pothole missing from one section.
Construction workers on a road with recently filled potholes
A man and woman looking seriously at the camera while standing in front of trucks parked at a depot.

thumbnail.altText: A drone shot shows a damaged dirt road alongside a brown river, with a massive pothole missing from one section.
Construction workers on a road with recently filled potholes
A man and woman looking seriously at the camera while standing in front of trucks parked at a depot.

items.title: 'Derek' the dingo a concern for some as he roams streets in outback town

children.content: An lone dingo, affectionately known as Derek, has been spotted in suburban streets, backyards and even in dog parks, raising some concern for Alice Springs residents.

image.altText: A dingo walking on a street

items.title: Former middle school teacher jailed for striking student in the face

children.content: The court heard Darroch Robinson struck the girl after she insulted him following the confiscation of her phone, causing her to fall into a table and break her teeth.

image.altText: A man in a shirt and tie leaves court

items.title: CLP, Labor have 'blood on their hands' after remote death, MLA says

children.content: An independent member of the Northern Territory parliament says successive NT governments "have blood on their hands", following the death of a 26-year-old man in a remote Arnhem Land community this week.

image.altText: A photo of an Indigenous man in a suit, speaking outside NT Parliament.

items.title: Uncle 'worried and scared' after fatally stabbing sistergirl, court hears

children.content: The partner of a man charged with the stabbing murder of a Tiwi Islands sistergirl tells the NT Supreme Court she saw him run outside "like a flash" before crying and repeatedly calling out "call the police".

image.altText: A First Nations man wearing mirrored sunglasses, pointing at himself.

items.title: The mystery of an outback 'catch and kill' squad during WWII has been solved

children.content: In the 1940s, a shadowy military unit was assembled to catch and kill Japanese soldiers on Australia's remote northern coast. The identity of the so-called Solomons Six was a mystery — until now.

gallery.altText: Four back and white headshots of men dressed in army uniform.
A black and white photo of a large group of men standing to attention in the bush
A black and white photo of a group of men studying pictures of aeroplanes on the deck of a boat.

thumbnail.altText: Four back and white headshots of men dressed in army uniform.
A black and white photo of a large group of men standing to attention in the bush
A black and white photo of a group of men studying pictures of aeroplanes on the deck of a boat.

items.title: How Trump turned on the first US-born Pope

children.content: US President Donald Trump described Pope Leo XIV's accession to the papacy as a meaningful moment for America. But now he has said the pontiff is hurting the Catholic Church, and is weak. Here's how their relationship has developed over the past year.

image.altText: A graphic photo of both Donald Trump and Pope Leo.

items.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate's renewed defamation claim names Australian YouTuber

children.content: An Adelaide YouTuber has been identified in a renewed defamation lawsuit self-described misogynist Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan have lodged with a US court against some of their online critics.

image.altText: Andrew Tate wearing sunglasses walks with brother Tristan

items.title: The myths of motherhood and the damage they do

children.content: I traced my family's maternal line from a home for abandoned children in Italy to my own wheelchair, confronting the myths we cling to about what makes a good mother — and the damage they do.

gallery.altText: Silhouette of a mother and young child facing away from each other with an image of an old Italian city in the background

thumbnail.altText: Silhouette of a mother and young child facing away from each other with an image of an old Italian city in the background

items.title: Crackdown on unregistered NDIS providers coming in bid to save $6b a year

children.content: NDIS Minister Mark Butler will announce reforms to the disability scheme on Wednesday that will aim to save more than $6 billion a year by 2035 — and the largely unregulated market of NDIS providers is at the top of his reform list.

image.altText: A tent with letters which read N D I S stands tall outside with trees in the background.

items.title: Automation is growing in Australia's mining industry — but what are the costs?

children.content: At Australia's largest gold mine, drivers are more likely to be found in a control room than a cabin, but researchers and unions say jobs and safety remain significant concerns.

image.altText: Newmont's gold mine near Boddington

items.title: Valuables stolen from boots of shipped cars, victims say police won't investigate

children.content: Elihu and Amanda De Valle say thousands of dollars worth of their belongings were stolen from the boot of their car when it was shipped from Melbourne to Perth, but police aren't able to investigate.

gallery.altText: A young couple standing in a kitchen

thumbnail.altText: A young couple standing in a kitchen

avatar.alt: A profile shot of ABC News journalist Lily Kristanto posing for a photo sitting as a desk with her hands clasped.

items.title: Police to interview driver after young man killed outside Supernova event

children.content: Organisers say a minute of silence will be held at midday for the family and friends of the victims before the event opens for another day.

image.altText: Bunches of flowers against a fence.

items.title: Devils coach a psychic medium who leans into the 'witchy stuff'

children.content: Georgia Walker describes herself as a psychic medium, which she combines with her skills as a counsellor and teacher, as head coach of the Tasmania Devils' women's VFL team.

image.altText: Woman on oval smiles for photo

items.title: Top national park in NSW revealed as state hits record number of visits

children.content: The latest NSW National Parks and Wildlife Service Park Visitation Survey shows there were 65.5 million visits to the state's parks over the 2024-25 financial year.

image.altText: a buchwalking group at the Blue Mountains National Park

items.title: Out at sea, 400 million barrels of oil buffer Japan from the fuel crisis

children.content: Japan has oil stockpile sites littered around the country, and as the world faces a fuel crisis, neighbours in Asia have been turning to Tokyo for assistance.

image.altText: An aerial view shows eight barges floating in the water just off Japan.

items.title: Doing disco or school pick-up, Jessie Ware is having more fun than ever

children.content: Whether making opulent, grown-up disco-pop or doing school pick-up, the glam British artist is having more fun than ever.

articles.title: There's a confidence in you I admire. Let's play, HARD!

children.content: Righteo champ, it's time to put down your toast and impress with your trivia prowess. Let's play, HARD!

image.altText: Tom Gleeson on the set of Hard Quiz gesturing behind him with a serious expression.

articles.title: The cast of Good News Week reflect on 30 years of the comedy game show

children.content: On April 19, 1999, comedy news game show Good News Week premiered, hosted by Paul McDermott, sparking nine seasons, a Gold Logie nomination and some first-chances for comedians.

image.altText: Paul McDermott, 30s, Julie McCrossin, 40s, and Mikey Robins, 30s, pose together on a stage littered with papers.

gallery.altText: Middle-aged brunette woman wearing fashionable skirt, bra & chain top poses with right hand upright

thumbnail.altText: Middle-aged brunette woman wearing fashionable skirt, bra & chain top poses with right hand upright

items.title: The skis being made from three types of Australian-grown timber

children.content: A couple in the NSW Snowy Mountains makes skis entirely from Australian-grown timber, though manufacturing for the snow industry in a "summer country" comes with its share of challenges.

image.altText: A man and a woman holding skis in a workshop setting, with a small baby.

items.title: 'She was us': History behind Joy, the first sex worker statue

children.content: During her short residency in Sydney, the statue of sex worker Joy had many detractors and was even vandalised. Thirty years later, she has returned, with her supporters hoping she will dismantle stereotypes that still plague the industry.

image.altText: Bronze statue of woman

items.title: 'No good reason' for schools to post photos of students on social media: expert

children.content: The independent girls' school in Brisbane is now only sharing what it says are unidentifiable shots of students' faces from the side or behind, due to concerns about image manipulation.

image.altText: Unidentifiable photos of students in green uniforms.

items.title: How a park on the Murray carries our bonds with the Western Front

children.content: A local Australian artist is exploring the bonds between a regional park and its namesake in France as part of an Anzac Day exhibit.

image.altText: Black and white photograph of Albury soldiers standing in front of a French building

items.title: Cowboy chases dream to make his board game an Aussie classic

children.content: Steve Hawe says he never set out to make a board game — but when inspiration struck, he couldn't ignore it.

gallery.altText: Steve Hawe sitting in front of hand drawn board game holding a little horse piece and smiling.
An illustrated board game with an ariel drawing of a campdraft arena and squares around it.
Two girls sitting laughing playing a hand drawn board game.

An illustrated board game with an ariel drawing of a campdraft arena and squares around it.
Two girls sitting laughing playing a hand drawn board game.

items.title: Six dead after gunman opens fire and barricades supermarket in Kyiv

children.content: At least six people have been killed and 14 are injured after a gunman opened fire in the streets of Kyiv.

image.altText: A police officer holds his hand out in front of the supermarket door.

items.title: Aurora chasers hit the road as WA activity slows

children.content: For Leonie and Dean McFaull, fuel prices aren't stopping them crossing the country to add to their capture of more than 100 auroras.

image.altText: Red light from the aurora shimmers off the Molloy Island canal.

items.title: Victoria's free public transport continues in May with half-price fares to follow

children.content: Public transport will be free in Victoria for another month, and fares will then be slashed by half until the rest of the year in a bid to curb fuel demand and provide economic relief.

image.altText: Passengers standing on a train platform as a train pulls in.

items.title: Chinese government-owned firm in bid to build Hobart stadium

children.content: A subsidiary of China's state-owned construction mega-company has been selected as one of two firms to tender for the right to build Hobart's new waterfront stadium.

image.altText: An aerial view od Hobart's waterfront, with an artist's impression of the stadium and new housing.

items.title: Fashion to frontline support: How this rack of clothes is changing lives

children.content: Women's shelters are having to get creative to acquire necessary funding such as turning fashion into sustaining frontline support.

gallery.altText: A woman smiling while searching through a rack of discounted, fashionable clothing.

thumbnail.altText: A woman smiling while searching through a rack of discounted, fashionable clothing.

items.title: Authors turn to self-publishing as big publishers look to cut costs

children.content: Is your dream to become the next JK Rowling or Stephanie Meyer? A new wave of writers is turning to self-publishing to turn fantasy into reality.

image.altText: Artwork of a female warrior with a sword in front of a fire-breathing dragon.

items.title: Crime scene declared after body found in burning car west of the Gold Coast

children.content: Emergency services were called to the scene just before 6am on Sunday morning where a body was found in a burning car.

image.altText: An aerial shot of a burned out car on the side of a road alongside a fire engine

items.title: Octopus species mysteriously disappears from Spencer Gulf

children.content: The once-abundant pale octopus has not been caught in lower Spencer Gulf for more than a year, and what happened to the species remains a mystery.

gallery.altText: An orange octopus on a blue fishing bin lip

thumbnail.altText: An orange octopus on a blue fishing bin lip
